title: Fixing Node.js Express Dynamic Route Parameter Validation

statement.markdown: Implement a Node.js Express middleware route /users/:id that validates the user ID. The ID is a positive integer. If the ID is valid, return a JSON response with the user's details; otherwise, return a 400 Bad Request response with the error message 'Invalid user ID'.
statement.inputFormat: A single string containing the user ID in the format '/users/:id'
statement.outputFormat: Either a JSON response with the user's details or a 400 Bad Request response with the error message 'Invalid user ID'
statement.notes: The user ID is a positive integer. Handle the case where the ID is not an integer or is not positive.

hints.text: Use Express middleware to validate the user ID

editorial.title: Fixing Node.js Express Dynamic Route Parameter Validation
editorial.intuition: Use Express middleware to validate the user ID
editorial.bruteForce: Check if the user ID is a positive integer using regular expressions
editorial.betterSolution: Use the parseInt() function to parse the user ID and check if it is a positive integer
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the Express middleware to validate the user ID and return a JSON response or a 400 Bad Request response
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Test cases validate the correctness of the solution
commonMistakes: Not checking if the user ID is a positive integer
Not handling the case where the ID is not an integer
